This classification is based on its botanical characteristics, such as its succulent stems and leaves. As a member of the Aizoaceae family, Ceraria pygmaea is closely related to other succulent plants native to Southern Africa. The genus Ceraria is also part of the subfamily Ruschioideae, which includes several genera of succulent plants. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects.
